agency: Arizona Department of Economic Security (DES), Family Assistance Administration (FAA); program = Nutrition Assistance (NA), Arizona's name for SNAP
abawd_enforced_2026_detail: Statewide enforcement is the FY2026 default. HR 1 (OBBBA) did NOT change the underlying work requirement (still avg 80 hrs/month), but it raised the ABAWD age range to 18-64 (effective benefit month 11/2025), narrowed exemptions, and limited geographic waivers to areas with unemployment over 10% (the 'insufficient jobs' / alternative-measures standard was eliminated). Arizona's prior FY2025 waiver (all Tribal reservation areas + all counties except Maricopa, under 'insufficient jobs') ended. For FY2026 Arizona requested a narrower geographic waiver covering ONLY Yuma County and certain Tribal reservation areas (10% unemployment criterion) on 09/26/2025; per the DES HR1 blog (snapshot 06/19/2026) that request was still PENDING USDA approval and no FY2026 Arizona waiver appears as approved on the USDA/FNA FY2025-2029 waiver-response list. Separately, an INDIVIDUAL exemption for Indians/Urban Indians/California Indians (per the Indian Health Care Improvement Act) was ADDED by HR 1, so tribal members are individually exempt regardless of any geographic waiver. Net: enforced statewide except (a) individually exempt populations incl. tribal members, and (b) a possible Yuma/tribal geographic waiver if/when approved.
hours_formula_detail: Arizona's CNAP policy manual (FAA2 'ABAWD Time Limits and Work Requirements', last revised 09/22/2025, AZ effective date 11/01/2025) states an ABAWD meets the work requirement by 'Working at least 20 hours per week (or an average of 80 hours per month). The hours can be met by any combination of any of the following: Self-employment. Paid work. In-kind work. Volunteer unpaid work.' Volunteer/community-service work counts HOUR-FOR-HOUR toward the flat 80-hour monthly requirement. There is NO SNAP-allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage formula for general volunteering. A benefit/min-wage calculation exists only in the separate, DES-ASSIGNED 'workfare' lane in the general NA work-registration context (FAA6); notably the ABAWD qualifying-activities list does not even include workfare among the ways to satisfy the ABAWD requirement (it lists work incl. volunteer, plus approved work programs: SNAP CAN, WIOA, TAA, veteran E&T). Therefore the platform's general nonprofit-volunteer lane = federal flat 80 hrs/month.
minimum_wage_note: Set to 0 because classification is federal_80 (no allotment/min-wage formula applies to general volunteering). For reference, Arizona's state minimum wage effective 01/01/2026 is $15.15/hour (Industrial Commission of Arizona, CPI-indexed; up from $14.70 in 2025), corroborated by multiple independent sources. Flagstaff ($17.85) and Tucson have higher local minimum wages, but these are not used in the ABAWD/volunteer calculation and are moot for AZ given the federal_80 classification.
qualifying_org_definition: Any organization or work program for which unpaid volunteer work is performed - e.g. a nonprofit, food pantry, library, park, government agency, or other public-service organization. Arizona's general volunteer lane requires NO pre-approved site list, MOU, or DES assignment (those apply only to the separate DES-assigned workfare lane). Verification is 'A written statement or collateral contact completed by the work program or organization for which the work is being done.'
verification_instrument: No state-issued form NUMBER exists for nonprofit certification of volunteer hours. Per the CNAP manual's Verification section, acceptable verification of the ABAWD work requirement is 'A written statement or collateral contact completed by the work program or organization for which the work is being done' (or paycheck stubs for paid work). The participant has primary responsibility for providing verification. Note: as of 09/02/2025 DES no longer accepts client self-statements; third-party (org-signed) documentation is required. FAA-1530A (ABAWD time-limit notice) and FAA-1786A (ABAWD/Work Registration Script) are agency notices, not volunteer-hours certification forms.
reporting_cadence_detail: ABAWD budgetary units are on Simplified Reporting. They are NOT required to affirmatively submit hours every month; they MUST report when the ABAWD's work hours DROP BELOW 20/week (avg 80/month). The reporting deadline is 'no later than the tenth calendar day of the month after the month the change occurred' (extended to the next workday if the 10th falls on a weekend/holiday). Verification of hours is provided at application, recertification, when questionable, or on request. (Prior file said reporting_cadence='monthly'; corrected to exception/change reporting per FAA2 manual.)
submission_deadline: By the 10th calendar day of the month after the month the change in hours occurred (extended to the following workday if the 10th is a weekend or holiday). Source: CNAP manual FAA2 'ABAWD Reporting Requirements'.
submission_mechanism_detail: Recipients submit verification/changes to DES via the MyFamilyBenefits / HEAplus online portal (myfamilybenefits.azdes.gov), in person at a local DES/FAA office, or by mail/fax. The org-signed written statement or a collateral contact serves as the verification document.
required_prestep_detail: No prestep gates the general volunteer lane. Volunteer unpaid work counts toward the 80 hours without prior DES assignment, an EDP/ECDP, county assignment, or provider registration. (Those gates apply only to the separate DES-assigned workfare and to mandatory SNAP CAN E&T components, which are distinct programs.) Work registration under 7 CFR 273.7 is a general SNAP administrative step (register for work at application) that is distinct from and does not block the ABAWD volunteer-hours pathway.
remote_stance_detail: Neither the CNAP manual nor DES public guidance addresses remote/virtual volunteering specifically. The qualifying-activity definition ('volunteer unpaid work' / work for an organization) contains no in-person requirement, but no source affirmatively endorses remote volunteering either. Treated as SILENT.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No Arizona DES/CNAP source restricts a certifying volunteer organization by state of incorporation or requires in-Arizona registration. The policy language refers generically to 'the work program or organization for which the work is being done.' Under the federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying organization may host volunteer work regardless of state of incorporation; absent any AZ restriction, an out-of-state 501(c)(3) (e.g. a CA-based nonprofit) should be operationally accepted, but no source affirmatively confirms this, so the field is SILENT.
geographic_waivers: Under HR 1 (OBBBA), geographic waivers are limited to areas with an unemployment rate over 10% (the 'insufficient jobs'/alternative-measures basis was eliminated). Arizona's broad FY2025 waiver (all Tribal reservation areas + all counties except Maricopa) ended. For FY2026 Arizona requested a narrower geographic waiver covering only Yuma County and certain Tribal reservation areas (10% unemployment), submitted 09/26/2025; per the DES HR1 blog this remained PENDING USDA approval as of mid-2026, and no approved FY2026 Arizona waiver appears on the USDA/FNA FY2025-2029 waiver-response list. Separately, tribal members hold an INDIVIDUAL ABAWD exemption (Indians/Urban Indians/California Indians per the Indian Health Care Improvement Act), added by HR 1.
county_variation_detail: Implementation is statewide via DES (no county-administered variation in rules). However, ABAWD ENFORCEMENT may vary geographically in FY2026: a pending geographic waiver could exempt Yuma County and certain Tribal reservation areas, and tribal members are individually exempt statewide. Hence county_variation_flag = true as it materially affects who must meet the requirement.
abawd_age_range_2026: 18 to 64 (exempt if under 18 or 65+), effective benefit month 11/2025 per HR 1 (statutory effective date 07/04/2025).
abawd_time_limit: 3 countable full months of NA benefits in a fixed 3-year period; current AZ 3-year period 01/01/2025 - 12/31/2027.
abawd_exemptions_2026: Medically certified mentally or physically unfit for work
Parent/budgetary-unit member responsible for a dependent child under age 14
Indians, Urban Indians, or California Indians (Indian Health Care Improvement Act) - ADDED by HR 1
(Removed by HR 1: former foster youth <24, homeless individuals, and veterans are no longer categorically exempt unless they qualify under another category)
conflicts_with_prior: abawd_enforced_2026: prior file said 'yes' (fully enforced statewide, no waivers). CORRECTED to 'partial': AZ requested a FY2026 geographic waiver for Yuma County + certain Tribal reservation areas (pending USDA approval as of mid-2026), AND HR 1 ADDED an individual exemption for Indians/Urban Indians/California Indians, so tribal members are individually exempt statewide. Enforcement is therefore not blanket-statewide.

geographic_waivers: prior file said 'None as of mid-2026 ... AZ fully enforced statewide.' CORRECTED: a narrower FY2026 geographic waiver (Yuma + tribal areas, 10% unemployment) was requested 09/26/2025 and was still pending; the new OBBBA standard is unemployment >10% only.

reporting_cadence: prior file said 'monthly'. CORRECTED to exception/change reporting (Simplified Reporting): ABAWDs report only when hours drop below the 80/month threshold, by the 10th of the following month - not an affirmative monthly hours submission.

(Confirmed, not changed) classification/hours_formula = federal_80 with general volunteer lane counting hour-for-hour toward 80; now verified VERBATIM from the CNAP manual FAA2 (volunteer unpaid work listed among the combinable activities), upgrading the basis for the 'high' confidence.
unresolved: Whether Arizona's FY2026 geographic waiver request (Yuma County + certain Tribal reservation areas, submitted 09/26/2025) was ultimately APPROVED by USDA/FNA - latest available DES source (06/19/2026 snapshot) shows it still pending; no approved FY2026 AZ waiver found on the USDA FY2025-2029 response list.
remote_stance: no AZ source addresses remote/virtual volunteering.

accepts_outofstate_nonprofit: AZ policy is silent on whether a certifying nonprofit must be AZ-incorporated; out-of-state 501(c)(3) acceptance is inferred from federal default but not affirmatively confirmed.
No state-issued volunteer-hours certification form NUMBER exists; org-signed written statement / collateral contact is the standard - confirmed, but means there is no fixed form layout to template.

Exact 2026 AZ minimum wage ($15.15) confirmed via multiple secondary sources and the ICA press-release URL, but the official ICA PDF could not be fetched directly (Cloudflare challenge); not material to AZ given federal_80 classification.
notes: VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22): Pulled the authoritative Arizona CNAP/CNAP-FAA policy manual pages (FAA2 ABAWD Time Limits & Work Requirements, last revised 09/22/2025 / AZ effective 11/01/2025; legal authorities 7 CFR 273.24(a)(b)(f), FNS Waiver, HR 1) and the DES HR1-impact blog (snapshot 06/19/2026) via the Internet Archive, since des.az.gov and dbmefaapolicy.azdes.gov are behind a Cloudflare challenge that blocks automated fetches. CLASSIFICATION = federal_80, now verified VERBATIM: the ABAWD work requirement is met by 'an average of 80 hours per month' met by 'any combination of: Self-employment. Paid work. In-kind work. Volunteer unpaid work.' General nonprofit/community-service volunteering counts hour-for-hour toward the flat 80; there is NO allotment-over-minimum-wage formula for the general volunteer lane (that formula belongs only to the separate DES-ASSIGNED workfare lane, which is not even listed among ABAWD qualifying activities). HR 1 (OBBBA) left the work requirement itself unchanged ('the work requirements for SNAP participants remain the same as they were before H.R. 1') but raised the age range to 18-64, narrowed exemptions (removed categorical foster-youth/homeless/veteran exemptions), added an individual tribal exemption, and limited geographic waivers to unemployment >10%. Verification = org-signed written statement or collateral contact; no state form number. Reporting = exception/change reporting (Simplified Reporting) with a 10th-of-following-month deadline, not affirmative monthly submission. Confidence HIGH: classification, hours, and verification instrument are all directly confirmed by the primary state policy manual.
